data.title: Analog semiconductor maker X-FAB flags high inventories in 2025
data.description:
Analog semiconductor manufacturer X-FAB flagged on Thursday persistent high inventories at its automotive customers, but sees recovery in industrial and medical demand in 2025.

The company, based in Erfurt, Germany, said the inventory correction in its automotive business will drag into 2025.

"Inventory corrections in the automotive supply chain, to which X-FAB has the highest exposure, only started in the second half of last year and should continue well into 2025," it said in its fourth quarter earnings report.

X-FAB joins peers in warning of automotive inventory corrections dragging into 2025, with customer Melexis noting on Wednesday car makers are delaying orders citing overstocking.

data.title: Embecta Corp. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Embecta Corp., a leading provider of diabetes care products, has released its Form 10-Q report for the first quarter of fiscal year 2024. The report outlines the company's financial performance and operational highlights, reflecting the challenges and strategic decisions faced by the company in a competitive market environment.

Financial Highlights

Embecta Corp. reported the following key financial metrics for the three months ended December 31, 2024:

  • Revenues: $261.9 million, a decrease of 5.6% from $277.3 million in the prior year period, driven by unfavorable changes in volume and foreign currency translation.
  • Gross Profit: $157.1 million, a decrease of 15.5% from $185.9 million, with gross profit as a percent of revenue at 60.0% compared to 67.0% in the prior year.
  • Operating Income: $28.7 million, a decrease of 36.9% from $45.5 million, reflecting higher costs and lower revenues.
  • Net Income: $0.0 million, a significant decrease from $20.1 million, primarily due to increased costs and decreased revenues.
  • Net Income per common share - Basic: $0.00, down from $0.35 in the prior year period.
  • Net Income per common share - Diluted: $0.00, down from $0.35 in the prior year period.

Business Highlights

Embecta Corp.'s business performance for the three months ended December 31, 2024, is summarized as follows:

  • Revenue Segments: The company's revenue was primarily driven by pen needles, which accounted for $191.1 million, followed by syringes at $28.4 million, safety products at $34.2 million, and contract manufacturing at $4.8 million.
  • Geographical Performance: The United States remained the largest market, contributing $141.7 million to the total revenue, while international markets accounted for $120.2 million. No individual country outside the United States generated more than 10% of total revenues.
  • Sales Units: The company experienced a decrease in sales volume, contributing to a $17.9 million decline in revenue, partially offset by a $2.9 million increase in contract manufacturing revenues.
  • Future Outlook: Embecta Corp. plans to refocus its investment on its core business, optimize free cash flow, and strengthen its balance sheet by paying down debt. The discontinuation of the patch pump program is expected to result in a restructuring plan with estimated pretax costs of $35.0 million to $45.0 million.

SEC Filing: Embecta Corp. [ EMBC ] - 10-Q - Feb. 06, 2025

data.title: OpenAI eyeing more data centers in Texas, other states for Stargate, Bloomberg News reports
data.description:
AI-infrastructure venture Stargate is nearing a decision on additional locations for data center campuses in Texas and is eyeing more projects in more than a dozen other U.S. states, Bloomberg News reported on Thursday.

Stargate, a joint venture between ChatGPT creator OpenAI, Oracle and SoftBank , plans to invest up to $500 billion to help the United States stay ahead of China and other competitors in the global AI race.

In January, U.S. President Donald Trump announced the private sector investment initiative to fund AI infrastructure, aiming to outpace rival nations in this critical business technology.

The initial data centers for the project are already under construction in Texas, Oracle Chairman Larry Ellison had said then.

OpenAI is far along in the process of selecting multiple locations in Texas for massive data center projects to support AI services, a company spokesperson told Bloomberg.

Microsoft-backed OpenAI is considering additional potential sites in 13 more states, with staff from OpenAI and SoftBank visiting locations in Pennsylvania, Wisconsin and Oregon this week, the report said.

OpenAI, Oracle and Softbank did not immediately respond to Reuters' requests for comment.

data.title: Hilton Looks to Add More Luxury Hotels to Its Portfolio — Market Talk
data.description: Hilton Worldwide is doubling down on luxury. CEO Chris Nassetta says on a call with analysts that luxury-lifestyle hotels accounted for roughly half of the company's system-wide openings in 2024, during which time it added a record 973 hotels, or nearly 100,000 rooms. The hotel operator now has more than 900 luxury-lifestyle hotels across the world. "Additionally, our luxury and lifestyle pipeline mix is nearly two times our existing supply, supporting continued growth in these important segments," Nassetta says. Shares rise 5% after Hilton logs higher profit and revenue in 4Q. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

data.title: Critical Metals Shares Fall Amid $22.5 Million Private Placement
data.description:
Critical Metals shares fell by nearly 30% in recent Thursday trading after the company said it was raising $22.5 million via a private investment in public equity financing program.

Critical Metals will issue 4.5 million shares at $5 each to investors, and an equal number of warrants that would be exercisable at $7 each.

The financing is expected to close Friday and will help raise funds to advance the Tanbreez rare earths project in Greenland and the Wolfsberg lithium project in Austria, Critical Metals said.

data.title: Leisure, Business and Group Travel Drives Hilton's 4Q Results — Market Talk
data.description: Demand for leisure, business and group travel drove Hilton Worldwide's revenue and profits higher in the recent quarter. Leisure occupancy was five points higher than pre-pandemic levels, and business travel continued to recover, with big tech and big banks meaningfully outperforming, CEO Chris Nassetta says on a call with analysts. Group travel also increased thanks to strong demand for company meetings and social events, he added. Overall revenue per available room, an important performance metric in the hospitality industry, notches a 3.5% year-over-year increase in 4Q. "As we look to the year ahead, we feel incrementally a bit better than we did a quarter ago," Nassetta says. Shares rise 4.8%. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

data.title: US Equity Indexes Mixed in Midday Trading
data.description:
US equity indexes traded mixed amid a quarterly earnings flood, with Amazon.com reporting after the bell and jobless claims increasing more than forecast.

The Nasdaq Composite rose 0.1% to 19,716.3, and the S&P 500 added 0.2% to 6,070.9 after midday Thursday. The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.4% to 44,695.4. Consumer staples and financials were among the gainers, while energy led the decliners intraday.

In company news, Ralph Lauren reported fiscal Q3 adjusted earnings and sales that beat the market's expectations. Shares soared nearly 12% intraday, among the top performers on the S&P 500.

Skyworks Solutions reported fiscal Q1 non-GAAP net income and net revenue late Wednesday that slumped from a year ago. Shares sank 24% intraday, the worst performer on the S&P 500.

Honeywell International has unveiled plans to split into three publicly-listed entities as part of a goal to optimize its portfolio and issued a downbeat full-year outlook, taking the shine out of its Q4 beat. Shares slumped 5.5% intraday, among the steepest decliners on the Nasdaq and the Dow.

Amazon.com will report quarterly results after the bell on Thursday.

In economic news, US initial jobless claims rose to 219,000 in the week ended Feb. 1 from an upwardly revised 208,000 in the previous week, compared with expectations for 213,000 in a survey of analysts compiled by Bloomberg.

Outplacement firm Challenger, Gray & Christmas said Thursday that companies planned to cut 49,795 jobs in January, up from 38,792 in December but below the 82,307 level in January 2024. The current month's level is the lowest for a January report since 2022.

Almost all US Treasury yields advanced, with the 10-year up 3.4 basis points to 4.45% and the two-year 2.5 basis points higher at 4.21%.

West Texas Intermediate crude oil futures fell less than 0.1% to $70.97 a barrel.

data.title: Ralph Lauren credits stronger-than-expected holiday season as stock rises on earnings boost
data.description:
Ralph Lauren Corp. (RL) shares are up 11.7% in premarket trading after the iconic clothing company's fiscal third-quarter results topped Wall Street's profit and sales estimates. The company reported better-than-expected holiday performance and raised guidance.

The company's adjusted fourth-quarter profit of $4.82 a share beat the FactSet consensus estimate of $4.53 a share. Revenue rose to $2.144 billion from $1.934 billion to beat the analyst revenue estimate of $2.014 billion.

Ralph Lauren also raised its full-year fiscal 2025 revenue guidance and now expects constant currency revenues to increase in a range of approximately 6% to 7%, up from its prior guidance of a 3% to 4% increase.

(This is a developing news story with updates to come.)

-James Rogers

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Paris Most Actives
PREVIEW: Amazon shares edge up; Q4 EPS, rev seen rising
data.description:
** Shares in Amazon.com , after hitting intraday record high on Tuesday, up 0.5% on Thursday ahead of Q4 report due after market close

** Wall Street consensus view is for AMZN to report adj EPS of $1.49 vs $1.00 in year-ago quarter on rev of $187.3 bln vs $169.96 bln in year-ago quarter, according to LSEG

** In last 8 quarters AMZN's EPS handily beat Street with last miss for Q3 2022 while rev met or beat consensus 7 times

** AMZN under pressure to deliver on lofty Q4 cloud computing expectations after lackluster Microsoft , Google results jolted faith in hefty AI investments

** AMZN options imply a 6.7% swing for the shares, in either direction, by Fri; over the last eight quarters, on average, the shares moved 6.6% the day after results, according to Trade Alert

** Stock last traded at $237.31 vs median PT of $255, up from $240 last month as per LSEG which shows 72 analyst ratings: 19 'strong buy,' 49 'buy' and 4 'hold' ratings

** Shares up 8.2% YTD after rising 44.4% in 2024 while Dow Industrials up 5.2% YTD after last year's 12.9% gain; S&P 500 consumer discretionary index up 2.9% YTD after 29.1% 2024 gain

data.title: Italian Stocks End at New 2008-High
data.description:
The FTSE MIB finished about 1.5% higher at 37,122 on Thursday, its highest since January 2008, in line with its European peers.

Traders seemed to have shrugged off concerns about the trade war and tariffs, instead focusing on a new set of corporate earnings.

Rumors that the long-awaited plan to end Russia's war in Ukraine will be presented next week by the Trump administration also boosted market sentiment.

Attention now shifts to the US jobs report, due tomorrow, which is likely to shape the Fed's future policy decisions.

In the business sector, banks stood out, particularly Banco BPM, Popolare di Sondrio, and MPS, which garnered attention following their earnings reports.
